TextView.android:text: 1. Type the IP subnet to scan, e.g. 192.168.0. or 192.168.\n2. Press SCAN.\n3. Wait for Scan completed. Do not touch anything until finished.
Available IP Addresses on your network:
This tab contains various experimental and advanced features.
Advanced debugging tools and system diagnostics.
Backup firmware partitions from the device
This runs on the device over SSH, reads /proc/mtd, creates MTD dumps under /tmp, generates SHA-256 checksums, archives the backup, and then prompts you to save the completed archive locally.
Restore firmware partitions to the device
Select a backup archive created by this tool. Each MTD partition will be uploaded and flashed back to the device, then the device will reboot.
Warning: Restoring firmware will overwrite all flash partitions including bootloader, kernel, and rootfs. A power failure or incorrect backup can permanently brick the device.

EditText.android:hint: Local backup path in app cache

CheckBox.android:text: I accept the risk of permanently bricking this device.

TextView.android:text: Select the correct bootloader for your SoC, confirm, then download, upload, and flash it on the device.
Warning: Replacing the bootloader can permanently brick the device. Proceed only if you accept the risk.

CheckBox.android:text: I accept the risk of bricking this device. This will overwrite /dev/mtd0 and erase /dev/mtd1.

TextView.android:text: Select a firmware source below. Choose one method — the selected card will highlight.
Application preferences are stored locally and applied across OpenIPC configuration screens.
When enabled in Companion: WFB, Camera, Telemetry, and Setup are hidden for camera workflows, and camera connections land on Firmware.
Advanced debugging tools and system diagnostics.
